name: Participant Recruitment Strategy
consequences: Immediate: High participant dropout rates → Systemic: Low data quality → Strategic: Failure to meet recruitment targets and subsequent project cancellation.
options: Traditional clinical referrals
Social media advertising targeting specific demographics
Collaboration with patient advocacy groups for grassroots outreach
review: Controls participant burden vs. researcher workload. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of social media advertising on participant privacy and data security.

name: Data Sharing Protocol
consequences: Immediate: Data sharing restrictions → Systemic: Limited access to research findings → Strategic: Missed opportunities for collaboration and knowledge exchange.
options: Open access data sharing with no restrictions
Limited data sharing only with approved researchers
Data sharing only after a specified delay or under strict confidentiality agreements
review: Controls data accessibility vs. intellectual property protection. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential consequences of open access data sharing on participant privacy and researcher reputation.

name: Staffing Model
consequences: Immediate: Inadequate staffing → Systemic: Increased workload for remaining staff → Strategic: Burnout and turnover among research team members.
options: Traditional full-time researcher model
Hybrid model combining full-time researchers with part-time technicians
Contractor-based staffing model with external experts
review: Controls labor costs vs. project timelines. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of contractor-based staffing on team cohesion and knowledge sharing.

name: Research Focus
consequences: Immediate: Misaligned research focus → Systemic: Inefficient use of resources → Strategic: Failure to meet project goals and objectives.
options: Exclusive focus on parasomnia diagnosis
Comprehensive approach including parasomnia prevention and treatment
Exploratory research into emerging sleep disorders
review: Controls research scope vs. resource allocation. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential consequences of exploratory research on project timelines and budget.

name: Facility Expansion Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Overcrowding and logistical challenges → Systemic: Increased costs and resource constraints → Strategic: Reduced participant comfort and satisfaction.
options: Gradual expansion based on demand
Rapid expansion to meet recruitment targets
Phased expansion focusing on specific research areas
review: Controls capacity growth vs. participant experience. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of rapid expansion on staff workload and participant satisfaction.

name: Data Analysis Tools
consequences: Immediate: Inefficient data analysis → Systemic: Delayed research findings → Strategic: Missed opportunities for publication and funding.
options: Traditional manual coding methods
Semi-automated event-triage tools
Advanced machine learning algorithms
review: Controls data processing efficiency vs. research productivity. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential consequences of advanced machine learning algorithms on participant privacy and researcher accountability.

name: Partnership Model
consequences: Immediate: Limited partnership opportunities → Systemic: Reduced access to resources and expertise → Strategic: Missed chances for collaboration and knowledge exchange.
options: Traditional academic partnerships
Industry partnerships with strict confidentiality agreements
Collaborative research networks with open data sharing
review: Controls partnership scope vs. intellectual property protection. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of collaborative research networks on participant privacy and researcher reputation.

name: Participant Engagement Tactic
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant satisfaction → Systemic: Enhanced data quality through improved participant cooperation → Strategic: Strengthened research validity and generalizability
options: Personalized pre-admission interviews to build rapport
Flexible compensation packages to incentivize participation
Incentivizing referrals from existing participants
review: Controls participant burden vs. data quality. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of varying participant motivations on data consistency.

name: Data Annotation Protocol
consequences: Immediate: Reduced annotation time → Systemic: Improved inter-rater reliability → Strategic: Enhanced scalability and reproducibility
options: Automated annotation tools leveraging machine learning
Structured annotation guidelines with clear criteria
Blinded, independent scoring to minimize bias
review: Controls data accuracy vs. scoring efficiency. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential trade-off between annotation speed and scorer expertise.

name: Sensor Calibration Procedure
consequences: Immediate: Improved sensor fidelity → Systemic: Enhanced data consistency → Strategic: Increased confidence in results
options: Regular, on-site calibration by manufacturer-trained technicians
Remote calibration via video conferencing and telepresence technology
Automated calibration protocols leveraging real-time sensor feedback
review: Controls sensor accuracy vs. logistical feasibility. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of varying environmental conditions on sensor performance.

name: Research Staff Training Plan
consequences: Immediate: Enhanced staff knowledge → Systemic: Improved data quality through accurate annotation → Strategic: Increased research validity and generalizability
options: Comprehensive, on-site training by manufacturer representatives
Remote training via online modules and virtual reality simulations
Peer-to-peer mentoring and experiential learning
review: Controls staff expertise vs. operational efficiency. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential trade-off between training time and research productivity.

name: Participant Flow Management
consequences: Immediate: Reduced participant turnover → Systemic: Improved data quality through consistent participation → Strategic: Enhanced research validity and generalizability
options: Personalized, pre-admission counseling to address concerns
Flexible scheduling to accommodate participant needs
Incentivizing retention through rewards and recognition
review: Controls participant satisfaction vs. operational efficiency. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of varying participant characteristics on flow management.

name: Data Sharing Policy
consequences: Immediate: Increased data accessibility → Systemic: Enhanced collaboration and knowledge sharing → Strategic: Strengthened research impact and validity
options: Open-access, public data repositories with clear usage guidelines
Restricted access to authorized researchers with approved protocols
Data sharing through collaborative partnerships and consortia
review: Controls data accessibility vs. intellectual property protection. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential trade-off between data sharing and participant privacy.

name: Research Governance Structure
consequences: Immediate: Improved research oversight → Systemic: Enhanced accountability and transparency → Strategic: Increased confidence in results and validity
options: External, independent review boards with clear protocols
Internal governance structures with defined roles and responsibilities
Collaborative, multi-stakeholder governance models
review: Controls research integrity vs. operational efficiency. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of varying stakeholder interests on governance.

name: Residential Unit Capacity Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant flow → Systemic: Higher data quality and more robust phenotyping analysis → Strategic: Enhanced research output and publications
options: Expand to full capacity (12 suites) immediately
Gradually increase capacity by 2-3 suites per year for the first two years
Implement a tiered participation model with priority access for high-yield participants
review: Controls participant enrollment vs. data quality. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on staffing ratios and technician workload.

name: Sensing Tier Model
consequences: Immediate: Increased monitoring resolution → Systemic: Enhanced event detection and triage → Strategic: Improved research findings and publications
options: Maintain the current tiered acquisition model with low-burden monitoring and scheduled enhanced nights
Introduce a new tier of high-resolution monitoring for participants with high parasomnia frequency
Adopt a continuous, real-time monitoring approach for all participants
review: Controls data quality vs. participant comfort. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant fatigue and dropout rates.
